heading: Annual reports on influence operations and campaigns in the United States by the Russian Federation

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: a Requirement
children.text: On an annual basis, the Director of the National Counterintelligence and Security Center shall submit to the congressional intelligence committees a report on the influence operations and campaigns in the United States conducted by the Russian Federation.
A description and listing of the Russian organizations and persons involved in influence operations and campaigns operating in the United States as of the date of the report.
An assessment of organizations that are associated with or receive funding from organizations and persons identified in paragraph (1), particularly such entities operating in the United States.
A description of the efforts by the organizations and persons identified in paragraph (1) to target, coerce, and influence populations within the United States.

An assessment of the activities of the organizations and persons identified in paragraph (1) designed to influence the opinions of elected leaders of the United States or candidates for election in the United States.

With respect to reports submitted after the first report, an assessment of the change in goals, tactics, techniques, and procedures of the influence operations and campaigns conducted by the organizations and persons identified in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: c Coordination
children.text: In carrying out subsection (a), the Director shall coordinate with the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency, the Director of the National Security Agency, and any other relevant head of an element of the intelligence community.

children.heading: Form
children.label: d Form
children.text: Each report submitted under subsection (a) shall be submitted in unclassified form, but may include a classified annex.
